info.name: OpenClaw Management API V2
info.description:
Management API for OpenClaw Cloud Service. Runs on port 9998.

Authentication: Bearer token (OPENCLAW_MGMT_API_KEY from .env)

Base URL: http://<VPS_IP>:9998

request.description: List configured MCP servers using `openclaw mcp list --json`.
Show one MCP server using `openclaw mcp show <name> --json`.
Create or update one MCP server using `openclaw mcp set <name> <json>`.
Remove one MCP server using `openclaw mcp unset <name>`.
List internal hooks using `openclaw hooks list --json`.
Check hook eligibility status using `openclaw hooks check --json`.
Inspect one hook using `openclaw hooks info <name> --json`.
Enable one hook using `openclaw hooks enable <name>`.
Disable one hook using `openclaw hooks disable <name>`.
Show the connected account identity using `openclaw directory self --json`.
Search peer/contact IDs using `openclaw directory peers list --json`.
List available groups/channels using `openclaw directory groups list --json`.
List members for a specific group using `openclaw directory groups members --json` with `--group-id`.
Run upstream `openclaw status --json` with optional `--all`, `--usage`, `--deep`, and `--timeout`. Use this when host-level `/api/status` looks healthy but you need deeper upstream diagnostics.
Fetch upstream usage cost summary through gateway RPC `usage.cost`.
Discover local or wide-area gateways using upstream `openclaw gateway discover --json`.
Run upstream `openclaw nodes status --json` with optional connected and last-connected filters. Useful for checking remote node fleet health and recent connectivity.
Run upstream `openclaw nodes list --json` to list pending and paired nodes. Supports the same gateway override and timeout query family as nodes status.
Run upstream `openclaw nodes describe --node <id> --json` for one node. Use when one peer looks inconsistent with fleet-level status.
Fetch the upstream model catalog through gateway method `models.list`.
Inspect configured model state via upstream `openclaw models status --json`.
Set the default model using `openclaw models set <model>`.
Set the default image model using `openclaw models set-image <model>`.
Read per-agent auth order override using `openclaw models auth order get --json`.
Set per-agent auth order override using `openclaw models auth order set`.
Clear per-agent auth order override using `openclaw models auth order clear`.
List model aliases using `openclaw models aliases list --json`.
Create or update a model alias using `openclaw models aliases add`.
Remove a model alias using `openclaw models aliases remove <alias>`.
List fallback models using `openclaw models fallbacks list --json`.
Add a fallback model using `openclaw models fallbacks add`.
Remove one fallback model using `openclaw models fallbacks remove <model>`.
Clear all fallback models using `openclaw models fallbacks clear`.
List image fallback models using `openclaw models image-fallbacks list --json`.
Add an image fallback model using `openclaw models image-fallbacks add`.
Remove one image fallback model using `openclaw models image-fallbacks remove <model>`.
Clear all image fallback models using `openclaw models image-fallbacks clear`.
List discovered plugins using `openclaw plugins list --json`.
Inspect all plugins using `openclaw plugins inspect --all --json`.
Inspect one plugin using `openclaw plugins inspect <id> --json`.
Enable one plugin using `openclaw plugins enable <id>`.
Disable one plugin using `openclaw plugins disable <id>`.

Get service info: domain, IP, pair dashboard URL, gateway token, masked management API key, runtime status, OpenClaw version, management API version/update status, and DNS/SSL state. `dashboardUrl` points to `/pair?token=...` on the management port.

originalRequest.description: Get service info: domain, IP, pair dashboard URL, gateway token, masked management API key, runtime status, OpenClaw version, management API version/update status, and DNS/SSL state. `dashboardUrl` points to `/pair?token=...` on the management port.

request.description: Get managed OpenClaw and Caddy unit status, including version and gateway port. The OpenClaw unit name follows `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` when configured, and may be managed through either system or user systemd depending deployment.

originalRequest.description: Get managed OpenClaw and Caddy unit status, including version and gateway port. The OpenClaw unit name follows `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` when configured, and may be managed through either system or user systemd depending deployment.

request.description: Get system info: hostname, IP, OS, uptime, CPU load, memory, disk usage, Node.js version, and OpenClaw CLI version.

originalRequest.description: Get system info: hostname, IP, OS, uptime, CPU load, memory, disk usage, Node.js version, and OpenClaw CLI version.

request.description: Public bootstrap redirect used during device pairing. Use the gateway token, not the management API key. It enables short-lived auto-polling and redirects to the gateway dashboard with the provided token in the URL fragment.
Fetch the public HTML login page served by the management API. This is not a JSON API endpoint.
Fetch the public HTML terminal UI page. Terminal command auth is handled client-side with the management API token.
Open the terminal SSE stream for one whitelisted command. This endpoint returns `text/event-stream`, not JSON, and authenticates via the `token` query parameter.
Validate configured login credentials and return the OpenClaw gateway token. This is the only auth route that does not require the management Bearer token.

originalRequest.description: Validate configured login credentials and return the OpenClaw gateway token. This is the only auth route that does not require the management Bearer token.

request.description: Create the single management login account stored in `.env`. This route requires the management API Bearer token.
Check whether a login user is configured and return the current username when present.
Replace the stored password hash for the configured login user. A login user must already exist.
Remove the stored management login credentials from `.env`.
Get current domain configuration, server IP, and SSL mode derived from the active environment/Caddy setup.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: Get current domain configuration, server IP, and SSL mode derived from the active environment/Caddy setup.

response.body:
{
  "ok": true,
  "domain": "openclaw.example.com",
  "ip": "1.2.3.4",
  "ssl": true,
  "selfSignedSSL": false,
  "acmeEmail": "admin@example.com",
  "sslIssuer": "letsencrypt",
  "sslIssuerHint": "Using Let's Encrypt as the primary ACME issuer.",
  "sslFallbackUsed": false
}

request.description: Check whether DNS, optional ACME email, and current issuer state are ready before applying a domain change.

response.name: Ready

originalRequest.description: Check whether DNS, optional ACME email, and current issuer state are ready before applying a domain change.

response.body:
{
  "ok": true,
  "requestedDomain": "openclaw.example.com",
  "domain": "openclaw.example.com",
  "domainValid": true,
  "serverIP": "1.2.3.4",
  "resolvedIPs": [
    "1.2.3.4"
  ],
  "dnsResolved": true,
  "dnsMatchesServer": true,
  "email": "admin@example.com",
  "emailProvided": true,
  "emailValid": true,
  "acmeEmailCleared": false,
  "ready": true,
  "issuerOrder": [
    "letsencrypt",
    "zerossl"
  ],
  "currentDomainMatch": true,
  "currentSslIssuer": "letsencrypt",
  "currentSslIssuerHint": "Using Let's Encrypt as the primary ACME issuer.",
  "warnings": [],
  "acmeDiagnostics": {
    "status": "ok",
    "summary": "No known ACME failure signature was detected in recent Caddy logs.",
    "findings": [],
    "suggestedActions": []
  },
  "acmeAssessment": {
    "status": "ok",
    "primaryCategory": "ready",
    "summary": "ACME validation looks healthy.",
    "issues": [],
    "suggestedActions": []
  },
  "recentCaddyAcmeLogs": [
    "... obtaining certificate ..."
  ]
}

request.description: Run live TCP and HTTP/HTTPS reachability checks for ports 80 and 443 after the normal ACME preflight validation.

response.name: Reachable

originalRequest.description: Run live TCP and HTTP/HTTPS reachability checks for ports 80 and 443 after the normal ACME preflight validation.

    "summary": "ACME validation looks healthy.",
    "issues": [],
    "suggestedActions": []
  }
}

request.description: Get the live SSL issuer state and recent ACME-related Caddy logs.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: Get the live SSL issuer state and recent ACME-related Caddy logs.

request.description:
Update domain and configure SSL via ACME (Let's Encrypt, fallback ZeroSSL). DNS must point to server IP. Validates DNS before applying. Rolls back on failure.

Parameters:
- domain (required): Domain name
- email (optional): Email for ACME notifications; send empty/null to clear it

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description:
Update domain and configure SSL via ACME (Let's Encrypt, fallback ZeroSSL). DNS must point to server IP. Validates DNS before applying. Rolls back on failure.

Parameters:
- domain (required): Domain name
- email (optional): Email for ACME notifications; send empty/null to clear it

response.name: DNS Error

Parameters:

request.description: Get configured OpenClaw release channel and the installed OpenClaw CLI version.

originalRequest.description: Get configured OpenClaw release channel and the installed OpenClaw CLI version.

request.description: Update the global OpenClaw package with npm and restart the managed OpenClaw unit. Runs asynchronously (returns 202). Check /api/status for progress.

originalRequest.description: Update the global OpenClaw package with npm and restart the managed OpenClaw unit. Runs asynchronously (returns 202). Check /api/status for progress.

request.description: Restart the managed OpenClaw unit.

originalRequest.description: Restart the managed OpenClaw unit.

request.description: Stop the managed OpenClaw unit.

originalRequest.description: Stop the managed OpenClaw unit.

request.description: Start the managed OpenClaw unit.

originalRequest.description: Start the managed OpenClaw unit.

request.description: Restart both managed services: OpenClaw and Caddy.

originalRequest.description: Restart both managed services: OpenClaw and Caddy.

request.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Delete all data + config, recreate from defaults. Requires {"confirm":"RESET"} in body. Keeps .env file (tokens preserved).

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Delete all data + config, recreate from defaults. Requires {"confirm":"RESET"} in body. Keeps .env file (tokens preserved).
DESTRUCTIVE: Delete all data + config, recreate from defaults. Requires {"confirm":"RESET"} in body. Keeps .env file (tokens preserved).

query.description: Service name: openclaw or caddy (default: openclaw)

request.description: Get recent systemd logs. Supports query params: lines (1-1000), service (openclaw|caddy).

query.description: Service name: openclaw or caddy (default: openclaw)

originalRequest.description: Get recent systemd logs. Supports query params: lines (1-1000), service (openclaw|caddy).

query.description: Set true to aggregate all configured agents

request.description: List stored OpenClaw sessions using the upstream `openclaw sessions --json` command. Supports `agent`, `store`, `active`, and `allAgents=true`.

query.description: Set true to aggregate all configured agents

originalRequest.description: List stored OpenClaw sessions using the upstream `openclaw sessions --json` command. Supports `agent`, `store`, `active`, and `allAgents=true`.

request.description: Run upstream session store maintenance. Body supports `store`, `agent`, `allAgents`, `dryRun`, `enforce`, `fixMissing`, and `activeKey`.
Create a backup archive via upstream `openclaw backup create --json`. Supports `output`, `dryRun`, `verify`, `onlyConfig`, and `includeWorkspace=false`.
Verify one backup archive through upstream `openclaw backup verify --json`. Requires `archive` path in the request body.
Return upstream cron scheduler status via gateway method `cron.status`.
List cron jobs via gateway method `cron.list`. Query `all=true` maps to `includeDisabled`.
Create a cron job using upstream gateway method `cron.add`. Send the same structured payload expected by the gateway method.
Patch one cron job via gateway method `cron.update`. You can send either a top-level `patch` object or raw patch fields in the body.
Run a cron job immediately via gateway method `cron.run`. Use `{ "mode": "due" }` or `{ "due": true }` for due-only execution.
Get current config: provider, model, API keys (masked), agents/gateway/browser settings.

originalRequest.description: Get current config: provider, model, API keys (masked), agents/gateway/browser settings.

request.description: Read one config value by dot path, similar to upstream `openclaw config get <path>`.
Run a management-layer config validation pass inspired by upstream `openclaw config validate`.

Change AI provider and model. Loads the selected built-in provider template or custom provider template, injects the gateway token, applies an optional model override, and restarts the managed OpenClaw unit. Built-in provider switches keep custom providers from the template directory merged into active config so upstream `models.mode=merge` behavior is preserved.

Provider may be any built-in provider key returned by `GET /api/providers` or any custom provider template name.

Model is optional for built-in providers — if omitted, the provider template's default model is used. Model is required for custom providers and should normally be sent as `provider/model-id`.

originalRequest.description:
Change AI provider and model. Loads the selected built-in provider template or custom provider template, injects the gateway token, applies an optional model override, and restarts the managed OpenClaw unit. Built-in provider switches keep custom providers from the template directory merged into active config so upstream `models.mode=merge` behavior is preserved.

Provider may be any built-in provider key returned by `GET /api/providers` or any custom provider template name.

Model is optional for built-in providers — if omitted, the provider template's default model is used. Model is required for custom providers and should normally be sent as `provider/model-id`.

request.description: Update the API key for built-in providers that support API-key auth (for example anthropic/openai/google/deepseek/groq). OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and must use `/api/config/chatgpt-oauth/*` endpoints. The management API normalizes pasted secrets by stripping embedded line breaks before saving to auth profiles, and also mirrors the secret into `.env` when targeting the main agent, then restarts OpenClaw.

originalRequest.description: Update the API key for built-in providers that support API-key auth (for example anthropic/openai/google/deepseek/groq). OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and must use `/api/config/chatgpt-oauth/*` endpoints. The management API normalizes pasted secrets by stripping embedded line breaks before saving to auth profiles, and also mirrors the secret into `.env` when targeting the main agent, then restarts OpenClaw.

request.description: Remove one API key for a provider that supports API-key auth. OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and must be managed with `/api/config/chatgpt-oauth/*` endpoints.
Remove one config value by dot path, similar to upstream `openclaw config unset <path>`.
Test an API key before applying for built-in providers that support API-key auth. OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and do not support API-key validation.

originalRequest.description: Test an API key before applying for built-in providers that support API-key auth. OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and do not support API-key validation.
Test an API key before applying for built-in providers that support API-key auth. OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected and do not support API-key validation.

request.description: List all built-in and custom providers, their available models, active status, default model, and masked credential presence.

Create a custom provider template, normalize and persist its API key when provided, switch the active config to that provider, and restart OpenClaw. Loopback/local `baseUrl` values such as `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1` or `http://localhost:11434/v1` may omit `apiKey` so OpenClaw can use its local-auth fallback semantics. Azure endpoints are normalized to the upstream config shape; Azure OpenAI endpoints are stored on `/openai/v1` with `api=openai-responses` and Azure-style `api-key` header auth.
List custom provider templates discovered in the config template directory, plus the current active provider/model.

Update one custom provider template and optionally append a new model or rotate its stored API key. When `apiKey` is provided, the management API normalizes pasted secrets before saving them. For loopback/local custom providers, sending an empty `apiKey` clears the stored key so runtime local-auth fallback can apply. Azure endpoints are re-normalized on update so stored `baseUrl`, `api`, and Azure `api-key` header auth remain aligned with upstream onboarding behavior.

Delete one custom provider template and remove its stored secrets. If the deleted provider is currently selected, the management API reloads the Anthropic built-in template into active config and returns the fallback built-in model so runtime config never points at a removed provider.

Append one model entry to a built-in or custom provider template. If that provider is already active in `openclaw.json`, the management API also updates the active config and restarts OpenClaw so the new model becomes available immediately.

Remove one model entry from a built-in or custom provider template by provider and model id. If that provider is active, the management API also updates the live config, restarts OpenClaw, and falls back the default selected model when the removed model was currently selected.
Get a flattened overview of the current OpenClaw config structure merged with provider template samples. Useful for building schema-aware UI controls and path pickers.

originalRequest.description: Get a flattened overview of the current OpenClaw config structure merged with provider template samples. Useful for building schema-aware UI controls and path pickers.

request.description: Inspect a single config path in the live config and in the merged schema/sample view. Sensitive values are masked.

originalRequest.description: Inspect a single config path in the live config and in the merged schema/sample view. Sensitive values are masked.

request.description: Deep-merge a partial object into openclaw.json. Existing endpoints remain available; this adds a more flexible schema-aware update path.

originalRequest.description: Deep-merge a partial object into openclaw.json. Existing endpoints remain available; this adds a more flexible schema-aware update path.

request.description: Set one config value by dot-path. Supports nested objects and array indexes. Set `remove: true` to delete a path.

originalRequest.description: Set one config value by dot-path. Supports nested objects and array indexes. Set `remove: true` to delete a path.

request.description: Apply persisted configuration and optionally restart `openclaw`, `caddy`, `all`, or `none`. Useful after batching multiple config edits.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: Apply persisted configuration and optionally restart `openclaw`, `caddy`, `all`, or `none`. Useful after batching multiple config edits.

request.description: Read the raw `openclaw.json` file content plus basic file metadata. Useful for audits, export, or advanced troubleshooting.

originalRequest.description: Read the raw `openclaw.json` file content plus basic file metadata. Useful for audits, export, or advanced troubleshooting.

request.description: List all messaging channels and their configuration status. Tokens are masked.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: List all messaging channels and their configuration status. Tokens are masked.

request.description:
Add or update a messaging channel token. Secrets are normalized before saving and the managed OpenClaw unit is restarted after applying.

Channels: telegram, discord, slack, zalo

For Slack, you can also pass `appToken` in the body. For Zalo, the route now defaults `dmPolicy` to `pairing` instead of `open`.

originalRequest.description:
Add or update a messaging channel token. Secrets are normalized before saving and the managed OpenClaw unit is restarted after applying.

Channels: telegram, discord, slack, zalo

For Slack, you can also pass `appToken` in the body. For Zalo, the route now defaults `dmPolicy` to `pairing` instead of `open`.

request.description:
Remove a messaging channel. Deletes the token from `.env` and restarts the managed OpenClaw unit.

Channels: telegram, discord, slack, zalo

originalRequest.description:
Remove a messaging channel. Deletes the token from `.env` and restarts the managed OpenClaw unit.

Channels: telegram, discord, slack, zalo

request.description: Fetch upstream channel status snapshot from gateway method `channels.status`.
List configured channels via upstream `openclaw channels list --json`.
Run upstream `openclaw channels capabilities --json` to inspect provider features, scopes, or target permissions.
Resolve user/group names to channel IDs through upstream `openclaw channels resolve --json`.
Read recent channel logs from the upstream gateway log file via `openclaw channels logs --json`.
List available skills across workspace, managed, and extra skill roots for the selected agent. Includes source, required binaries, and per-skill config overrides.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: List available skills across workspace, managed, and extra skill roots for the selected agent. Includes source, required binaries, and per-skill config overrides.

request.description: Get summarized skill status for one agent, including workspace roots, watcher settings, install preferences, and disabled skills.

originalRequest.description: Get summarized skill status for one agent, including workspace roots, watcher settings, install preferences, and disabled skills.

request.description: Search ClawHub skills using upstream `openclaw skills search --json`. Helpful when validating whether a skill exists before enabling or debugging it.
Run upstream `openclaw skills check --json` to see which skills are ready versus missing requirements. Pair with `/api/skills/bins` when comparing hosts.
Enqueue a system event through upstream gateway method `system-event`. Use `mode` of `now` or `next-heartbeat`.
Read the last upstream heartbeat event via gateway method `last-heartbeat`. Helpful for verifying that background liveness signals are still flowing.
Enable upstream heartbeats through gateway method `set-heartbeats` with `enabled=true`. Useful after temporary maintenance or debugging sessions.
Disable upstream heartbeats through gateway method `set-heartbeats` with `enabled=false`. Use cautiously because some health workflows rely on heartbeat activity.
List system presence entries via upstream gateway method `system-presence`. Use alongside heartbeat and nodes status when diagnosing remote availability.
Reload runtime secret references using upstream `openclaw secrets reload --json`. Run this after changing secret files, env-backed refs, or external secret providers.

query.description: When true, upstream audit may return a non-zero exit code if findings exist
Allow exec SecretRef checks during audit

request.description: Run upstream `openclaw secrets audit --json` to inspect plaintext secrets, unresolved refs, and precedence drift. Review the JSON body even if the upstream CLI reports findings through a non-zero exit code.

query.description: Enable best-effort live gateway probe checks

request.description: Run upstream `openclaw security audit --json` for security foot-gun detection without applying fixes. Use `deep=true` for broader checks when longer runtime is acceptable.
Aggregate binary dependencies declared by all discovered skills for the selected agent.

originalRequest.description: Aggregate binary dependencies declared by all discovered skills for the selected agent.

request.description: Get full details of a skill, including parsed frontmatter, metadata, and the raw SKILL.md content.

originalRequest.description: Get full details of a skill, including parsed frontmatter, metadata, and the raw SKILL.md content.

request.description: Update `skills.entries.<skillKey>` in `openclaw.json` with enabled state, API key, env overrides, and custom config fields. API keys are normalized like upstream `skills.update`, and env values are trimmed string values; send an empty string to remove one env key.

originalRequest.description: Update `skills.entries.<skillKey>` in `openclaw.json` with enabled state, API key, env overrides, and custom config fields. API keys are normalized like upstream `skills.update`, and env values are trimmed string values; send an empty string to remove one env key.

body.raw:
{
  "agentId": "main",
  "skillKey": "custom-vps-audit",
  "title": "Custom VPS Audit",
  "description": "Detailed skill for auditing VPS OpenClaw setup health and configuration.",
  "summary": "Use this skill when the user asks for a structured OpenClaw VPS audit, deployment verification, or support checklist.",
  "metadata": {
    "emoji": "🛠️",
    "homepage": "https://example.com/internal-skill-docs",
    "skillKey": "custom-vps-audit",
    "requires": {
      "bins": ["openclaw", "curl"],
      "config": ["gateway.controlUi.enabled"]
    },
    "primaryEnv": "OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"
  },
  "activation": [
    "Use when the user asks for a VPS audit or operational readiness report.",
    "Use when support staff need a repeatable troubleshooting checklist."
  ],
  "inputs": [
    "Deployment host or domain",
    "Relevant agentId if not using the default agent",
    "Any required credentials or API scope limitations"
  ],
  "workflow": [
    "Review service status, system metrics, domain/SSL state, and active provider config.",
    "Check skills, channels, env overrides, and bindings that affect routing.",
    "Summarize risks, missing config, and recommended next actions in priority order."
  ],
  "outputs": [
    "Health summary",
    "Detected issues",
    "Recommended remediation steps"
  ],
  "commandExamples": [
    "Audit the current OpenClaw VPS deployment.",
    "Check if the gateway token and provider setup look healthy."
  ],
  "configNotes": [
    "Document required `skills.entries.custom-vps-audit` overrides here.",
    "Mention any environment variables injected for host-only execution."
  ],
  "safetyNotes": [
    "Do not rotate keys automatically.",
    "Ask before any destructive change or service restart."
  ],
  "troubleshooting": [
    "If metrics disagree, compare `/api/status`, `/api/system`, and recent logs.",
    "If a provider is configured but health fails, test the key separately before changing it."
  ]
}

request.description: Create a detailed custom workspace skill under the selected agent workspace. This endpoint is designed to capture rich custom-skill documentation, metadata, guardrails, and workflow detail in one request.

body.raw:
{
  "agentId": "main",
  "skillKey": "custom-vps-audit",
  "title": "Custom VPS Audit",
  "description": "Detailed skill for auditing VPS OpenClaw setup health and configuration.",
  "summary": "Use this skill when the user asks for a structured OpenClaw VPS audit, deployment verification, or support checklist.",
  "metadata": {
    "emoji": "🛠️",
    "homepage": "https://example.com/internal-skill-docs",
    "skillKey": "custom-vps-audit",
    "requires": {
      "bins": ["openclaw", "curl"],
      "config": ["gateway.controlUi.enabled"]
    },
    "primaryEnv": "OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"
  },
  "activation": [
    "Use when the user asks for a VPS audit or operational readiness report.",
    "Use when support staff need a repeatable troubleshooting checklist."
  ],
  "inputs": [
    "Deployment host or domain",
    "Relevant agentId if not using the default agent",
    "Any required credentials or API scope limitations"
  ],
  "workflow": [
    "Review service status, system metrics, domain/SSL state, and active provider config.",
    "Check skills, channels, env overrides, and bindings that affect routing.",
    "Summarize risks, missing config, and recommended next actions in priority order."
  ],
  "outputs": [
    "Health summary",
    "Detected issues",
    "Recommended remediation steps"
  ],
  "commandExamples": [
    "Audit the current OpenClaw VPS deployment.",
    "Check if the gateway token and provider setup look healthy."
  ],
  "configNotes": [
    "Document required `skills.entries.custom-vps-audit` overrides here.",
    "Mention any environment variables injected for host-only execution."
  ],
  "safetyNotes": [
    "Do not rotate keys automatically.",
    "Ask before any destructive change or service restart."
  ],
  "troubleshooting": [
    "If metrics disagree, compare `/api/status`, `/api/system`, and recent logs.",
    "If a provider is configured but health fails, test the key separately before changing it."
  ]
}

originalRequest.description: Create a detailed custom workspace skill under the selected agent workspace. This endpoint is designed to capture rich custom-skill documentation, metadata, guardrails, and workflow detail in one request.

body.raw:
{
  "agentId": "main",
  "title": "Custom VPS Audit",
  "summary": "Expanded workflow for triaging deployment issues, provider drift, and missing runtime dependencies.",
  "workflow": [
    "Capture baseline system and service status.",
    "Inspect gateway config, skills, channels, and bindings.",
    "Return a prioritized findings list with remediation suggestions."
  ],
  "safetyNotes": [
    "Never delete data without explicit approval.",
    "Mask all keys and secrets in the response."
  ]
}

originalRequest.description: Update an existing workspace custom skill by regenerating the `SKILL.md` content with richer workflow and safety details.

request.description: List custom workspace skills for an agent with parsed sections, validation results, and optional raw markdown content.
Get rich detail for one custom workspace skill, including parsed sections, timestamps, validation issues, and optional raw content.

body.raw:
{
  "skillKey": "custom-vps-audit",
  "content": "---
name: custom-vps-audit
description: "Detailed custom skill for VPS audits."
---

# Custom VPS Audit

Use this skill when the user asks for an OpenClaw VPS audit.

## When to Use

- Use when the user requests a readiness or troubleshooting audit.

## Inputs to Collect

- Target environment and hostname

## Execution Workflow

- Check service status
- Review config and channels
- Summarize findings

## Expected Output

- Audit summary

## Safety and Guardrails

- Ask before destructive changes

## Troubleshooting

- Compare status, config, and logs when results conflict
"
}

request.description: Validate custom SKILL.md markdown before saving it. Returns parsed sections and any missing/empty sections.
Render a detailed custom SKILL.md document from structured JSON input without writing it to disk.
Delete a custom workspace skill directory for the selected agent.
Get upstream update channel and version status using the `openclaw update status --json` CLI surface.
Run the upstream update pipeline and schedule restart only on success.
Get upstream memory index and embedding-provider status using `openclaw memory status --json`.
Trigger upstream memory reindexing using `openclaw memory index --force --json`.
Search upstream memory entries using `openclaw memory search --json`.
List devices using the local CLI fallback `openclaw devices list`. This is separate from the upstream gateway pairing APIs below.

Approve the latest pending device request by request ID using the local CLI fallback `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`. Use the `requestId` returned by `openclaw devices list`, not a paired `deviceId`.
List pending and paired devices through upstream `device.pair.list`.
Approve a device pairing request via `device.pair.approve`.
Reject a device pairing request via `device.pair.reject`.
Remove a paired device using upstream `device.pair.remove`.
Rotate a device auth token through upstream `device.token.rotate`.
Revoke a device auth token through upstream `device.token.revoke`.

Get a management-layer readiness summary for memory embeddings based on the current default agent provider and configured credentials. OAuth-only chat providers (for example `openai-codex`) do not satisfy embeddings readiness by themselves.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: Get a management-layer readiness summary for memory embeddings based on the current default agent provider and configured credentials. OAuth-only chat providers (for example `openai-codex`) do not satisfy embeddings readiness by themselves.

request.description: List configured agents, default status, model assignments, and auth-profile counts.
Create a new managed agent with workspace and agent directories initialized automatically.
Get one agent with effective default status, masked API keys, and auth-profile presence.
Update editable agent metadata such as `name`, `model`, `workspace`, or `agentDir`.
Delete a non-default agent. By default the management API also removes the agent's workspace, agent runtime directory, and session transcript directory; send `deleteData=false` to keep those files.
Mark an agent as the default agent used by the management layer.
Read masked provider API keys stored for one agent via auth profiles.

Store or replace one provider API key for a specific agent and restart OpenClaw. Pasted secrets are normalized before being written to auth profiles. OAuth-only providers such as `openai-codex` are rejected here and must use `/api/config/chatgpt-oauth/*` endpoints.
List supported upstream-style agent workspace files such as `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `BOOT.md`, `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and `MEMORY.md` for one agent. Returns existence and file metadata when present.

originalRequest.description: List supported upstream-style agent workspace files such as `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `BOOT.md`, `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and `MEMORY.md` for one agent. Returns existence and file metadata when present.

request.description: Read the content of one supported agent workspace file. Only canonical OpenClaw workspace file names are allowed, and symlink targets outside the agent workspace are rejected.

response.name: Success

originalRequest.description: Read the content of one supported agent workspace file. Only canonical OpenClaw workspace file names are allowed, and symlink targets outside the agent workspace are rejected.

request.description: Create or update one allowed workspace file for an agent. The server rejects unsupported names, path traversal attempts, and symlink targets that escape the agent workspace.

originalRequest.description: Create or update one allowed workspace file for an agent. The server rejects unsupported names, path traversal attempts, and symlink targets that escape the agent workspace.

request.description: List current routing bindings from management config. Each binding maps one `match` object to an `agentId`.

originalRequest.description: List current routing bindings from management config. Each binding maps one `match` object to an `agentId`.

request.description: Create a new routing binding. `match.channel` is required and `agentId` must reference an existing managed agent.
Update one binding by index. You can replace `agentId`, `match`, or both. When sending `match`, `match.channel` is still required.
Delete one routing binding by numeric index.
Start the ChatGPT OAuth flow for one agent. Response includes `sessionId` and `oauthUrl`; open `oauthUrl` in a browser, then send the final redirect URL to the complete endpoint.

Complete OAuth after browser login. Supply the `sessionId` from the start call and either the full redirect URL containing both `code` and `state` (query or hash params), or a compact `code#state` value (for CSRF validation). When `switchProvider` is true (default), the management API loads the built-in `openai-codex` template into active config, applies the selected model override, and restarts OpenClaw.
Refresh the stored OAuth token for one agent using the saved refresh token.

query.description: Optional managed agent id; defaults to main

request.description: Inspect whether an OAuth token exists for the selected agent, including refresh-token presence and expiry metadata.
Get all environment variables from .env file. Sensitive values (containing TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD) are masked.

originalRequest.description: Get all environment variables from .env file. Sensitive values (containing TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD) are masked.

request.description: Set an environment variable in .env. Key must be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Cannot modify OPENCLAW_MGMT_API_KEY. Restart service for changes to take effect.

originalRequest.description: Set an environment variable in .env. Key must be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Cannot modify OPENCLAW_MGMT_API_KEY. Restart service for changes to take effect.

request.description: Remove an environment variable from .env. Protected keys (OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, OPENCLAW_MGMT_API_KEY, OPENCLAW_VERSION, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT) cannot be removed.

originalRequest.description: Remove an environment variable from .env. Protected keys (OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, OPENCLAW_MGMT_API_KEY, OPENCLAW_VERSION, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT) cannot be removed.

request.description:
Execute an OpenClaw CLI command directly on the host runtime.

Runs: `openclaw <command>` with `HOME=/opt/openclaw`.

Security: Characters ; & | ` $ ( ) { } are blocked.

Examples:
- models scan
- config get
- config set agents.defaults.model.primary anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
- channels list
- gateway status

originalRequest.description:
Execute an OpenClaw CLI command directly on the host runtime.

Runs: `openclaw <command>` with `HOME=/opt/openclaw`.

Security: Characters ; & | ` $ ( ) { } are blocked.

Examples:
- models scan
- config get
- config set agents.defaults.model.primary anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
- channels list
- gateway status

Execute an OpenClaw CLI command directly on the host runtime.

Runs: `openclaw <command>` with `HOME=/opt/openclaw`.

Examples:
